data.title: Wall Street rises after PCE index data comes in line
data.description:
Wall Street rises on Friday after the Federal Reserve’s preferred measure of inflation gauge showed results in line with expectations, easing investor concerns over price pressures and bolstering market sentiment.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) has pushed up by 0.6%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) is +0.4%, and the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) trades higher by 0.1%.

From a sector-by-sector stance, eight of the 11 S&P groups are in positive trading territory, with Energy leading the way. At the other end of the spectrum, Info Tech has suffered the most. 

U.S. Treasury yields remain mostly unchanged. The longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y)  is up 1 basis point to 4.19% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) is flat at 3.66%.

On the economic front, the Core Personal Consumption Expenditure Price Index (excludes food and energy) came in at +0.2% vs. +0.2% consensus and +0.2% prior (revised from +0.3%), and +2.9% Y/Y vs. +2.9% consensus and +2.9% in July. 

The PCE Price Index came in at +0.3% vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior, and +2.7% Y/Y vs. +2.7% consensus and +2.6% prior. 

“Friday's PCE for August was as expected, and although it is still firmly above the Federal Reserve's 2% target, we believe the central bank remains on track to cut interest rates again at its next meeting in October, since it's clear that inflation is stable enough to handle lower interest rates,” Clark Bellin, president and chief investment officer of Bellwether Wealth stated. 

Furthermore, the September U.S. Consumer Sentiment Index also arrived. 

On tariff news, President Donald Trump announced sweeping new tariffs, including 100% on branded and patented pharmaceutical drugs, to take effect on October 1, with an exemption if the company is actively building a new manufacturing facility in the U.S. 

“The 100% pharmaceutical tariff applies only to branded drugs, and constructing a factory may lead to an exemption. Many pharmaceutical companies have facilities in the U.S., so it may be relatively easy to superficially expand those facilities to avoid tariffs being applied,” UBS' Paul Donovan said.

data.title: Lockheed Martin eyes expansion in Europe, touting Thaad missile defense - Handelsblatt
data.description:
Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) is seeking to market its Thaad missile defense system to European buyers, a top executive told German newspaper Handelsblatt on Friday, as European governments boost military spending in response to Russian threats, including recent incidents involving Russian drones entering NATO airspace.

Lockheed Martin International (NYSE:LMT) head Michael Williamson said in an interview that he sees the greatest growth prospects in markets outside the U.S., such as Europe, over the next five years, and that the company is in negotiations with potential customers for the Thaad system, which costs more than €1 billion.

Thaad could be introduced to Europe as part of the European Skyshield Initiative, a project to build a ground-based integrated European air defense system which includes anti-ballistic missile capability, as an alternative to Israel's Arrow 3 system, according to the report.

Williamson said Lockheed's (LMT) partnership with Rheinmetall (OTCPK:RNMBF) (OTCPK:RNMBY) is a key to securing future contracts in Germany, with Handelsblatt saying the cooperation creates jobs and opens doors to political decision-makers.

Northrop Grumman (NOC), which currently generates 85% of its revenues in the U.S., also sees opportunities in Germany and Europe, with Joseph Guastella, the company's VP leading European operations, telling Handelsblatt that its partnerships in the region "bring benefits for both sides - for Germany and the United States, as well as for NATO as a a whole."

data.title: European indexes rebound as investors shrug off fresh tariff fears
data.description:
Update: The post reflects European markets closing indices.

London (UKX) +0.77% to 9,285. 

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.87% to 23,739. 

France (CAC:IND) +0.97% to 7,871.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) turned 0.78% higher to 554.52, snapping two sessions of losses. Investors largely shrugged off new U.S. tariff announcements from Trump, including a 100% levy on drugs, 25% on heavy trucks and more. Sector-wise, financials led gains while healthcare lagged. 

The European Commission is reportedly planning to impose steep tariffs of 25%–50% on Chinese steel imports and related products in the coming weeks.

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury up 2 basis points at 4.19%.

Germany's 10-year yield was unchanged at 2.75%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 2 basis points to 4.75%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

Seeking Alpha Editor Khushi Singh contributed to this article.

data.title: Trump goes after Tylenol again in Truth Social post
data.description:
• President Trump on Friday warned pregnant women not to take Tylenol (acetaminophen) "unless absolutely necessary," repeating comments he made on Sept. 22 at the White House. 
  • In a post on his Truth Social account, Trump also also said Tylenol, which is manufactured by Kenvue (NYSE:KVUE), should not be given to young children "for virtually any reason."
  • The president also repeated his Sept. 22 suggestion that the measles, mumps, and rubella vaccine (MMR) be given as three separate shots, the chicken pox shot should be given separately, and a hepatitis B shot be given only when an individual is 12 years or older. 
  • Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: ByteDance expected to play big role in TikTok USA which would be two companies - Reuters
data.description:
ByteDance (BDNCE) will have a bigger than expected role in TikTok U.S., as the new structure of the American entity keeps the Beijing-based parent significantly involved, Reuters reported on Friday, citing three sources.

Sources told Reuters that TikTok U.S. would be divided into two companies. The first company would be the joint venture, which includes Oracle (ORCL), Silver Lake, and other parties who will own and handle U.S. user data and algorithms. 

ByteDance would still be the single largest minority shareholder in this JV but will relinquish control of the app’s data, content, and algorithm.

The second company, which will be wholly owned by ByteDance, will control revenue-generating business operations such as e-commerce and advertising, the report said, citing sources.

The structure is still under discussion and could change, sources told Reuters.

Chinese media reports on Friday from news outlets LatePost and Caixin, which were taken down later, described a two-part structure in which ByteDance will continue to own the part of TikTok U.S. that will be responsible for e-commerce, branding operations, and interconnection with international operations, while a separate new joint venture will handle the user data and algorithm.

President Donald Trump on Thursday cleared a proposal to allow TikTok to continue operating in the U.S. in a deal that Vice President JD Vance said would value the app's U.S. operations at around $14B.

data.title: Perplexity unleashes Search API to challenge Google
data.description:
Perplexity has launched Perplexity Search API, or search application programming interface, that provides developers access to the same global-scale infrastructure that powers Perplexity's public answer engine.

The index includes hundreds of billions of web pages, updated in real time, which developers need to build new applications, especially in the age of artificial intelligence. It's the first large-scale, competitive search engine developers could use in place of Google (NASDAQ:GOOG)(NASDAQ:GOOGL) or even Microsoft's (MSFT) Bing in building applications.

"When it comes to AI, context is king," Perplexity said in a blog post. "It is insufficient to operate simply at the document level. Our indexing and retrieval infrastructure divides documents up into fine-grained units. These sub-document units are individually surfaced and scored against the original query parameters, allowing our API to return documents with the most relevant snippets already ranked. This means less preprocessing, faster integration, and more valuable downstream results." 

Perplexity also released a new SDK, or software development kit, that shows how the startup designed, optimized, and evaluated its Search API. 

In August, Perplexity made a $34.5B bid to buy Google's Chrome web browser.

data.title: Klarna stock falls to below IPO price amid four-day fintech slump
data.description:
Fintech stocks are on track for a fifth straight day of declines as stronger-than-expected economic data damps optimism for Fed rate cuts. With the slump, Klarna (NYSE:KLAR) stock, which made its trading debut less than three weeks ago, dropped to below its IPO price of $40.

In midday Friday trading, Klarna (NYSE:KLAR) fell 5.8% to $38.97. The Global X FinTech ETF (FINX) slipped 0.4%, and ARK Fintech Innovation ETF (ARKF) dipped 0.3%.

Fintech stocks are particularly sensitive to macro factors, such as regulatory developments or interest rates, Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Diksha Gera told Bloomberg News. "Any hint of slower-than-expected cuts or yields rising could pressure valuations and sentiment as higher yields can increase their borrowing costs," she said, adding that Klarna's (NYSE:KLAR) IPO price was already on the rich side.

Chime Financial (CHYM), another fintech that surged on its first day of trading, fell 2.2% on Friday to $21.07, below its IPO price of $27.

Affirm Holdings (AFRM), a direct competitor with Klarna in the buy now, pay later space, dropped 2.6%. 

Other fintech sliding include: Block (XYZ) -1.4%, Dave (DAVE) -2.9%, SoFi Technologies (SOFI) -1.5%, Adyen (OTCPK:ADYEY) -1.7%, and Green Dot (GDOT) -1.7%.

data.title: Ciena rises as Rosenblatt upgrades on AI opportunity
data.description:
• Ciena (NYSE:CIEN) shares rose 3% on Friday as Rosenblatt upgraded the telecom equipment company, citing additional opportunities in artificial intelligence. 
  • “We are upgrading CIEN to Buy on the Scale Across opportunity to network multiple AI
    Data Centers into clusters. So far, Ciena has won one such deal with a Hyper Scaler to
    connect two 100,000 GPU Data Centers 100km apart using WaveLogic 6 Nano 800G ZR pluggables,” Rosenblatt analyst Mike Genovese wrote in a note to clients.
  • Genovese, who attended the company's investor day, added that the aforementioned hyperscaler is installing 20 petabytes of capacity, and thus boosting Ciena's
    development of multi-rail amplifiers. 
  • “We estimate the revenues for this deal to be ~$200mn including 800G ZR and ZR+ pluggables and common equipment like amplifiers,” Genovese added. “We believe the scale across opportunity is just getting started, and Ciena is likely to win many more deals to interconnect Data Centers into clusters using pluggables or embedded DWDM depending on distance and performance requirements.”

data.title: MEG Energy holders should vote in favor of Cenovus takeover, ISS says
data.description:
MEG Energy (OTCPK:MEGEF) shareholders should vote in favor of its planned sale to Cenovus Energy (NYSE:CVE), proxy adviser ISS recommended. 

“It is difficult to conclude with certainty that there is a more compelling offer available, or that one will emerge," ISS said, according to Bloomberg review of the ISS report. 

The recommendation comes as Cenovus Energy (NYSE:CVE) has been battling with Strathcona Resources (OTCPK:STHRF) to purchase MEG  Energy. Cenovus Energy agreed to buy MEG Energy in a cash and stock deal valued at C$7.9 billion (~US$5.7 billion) including debt, last month, though Strathcona has raised its own bid, though the offer has been rejected. A shareholder vote is scheduled for Oct. 9. 

“MEG shareholders will have an opportunity to participate in the upside potential of the combined company, albeit only partially and there appears to be downside risk of non-approval," ISS said in its report.

data.title: KNOT Offshore Partners outlines $895M contract backlog and accelerates fleet expansion through vessel acquisitions and buybacks
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: KNOT Offshore Partners LP (KNOP) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Derek Lowe, CEO & CFO, opened the call by highlighting key financial results and operational headlines for the second quarter, reporting revenues of $87.1 million, operating income of $22.2 million, net income of $6.8 million, and adjusted EBITDA of $51.6 million. Available liquidity at quarter end totaled $104 million, reflecting a $4 million increase from the previous quarter, with $66.3 million in cash and $38.5 million in undrawn credit capacity. Utilization was 96.8%, factoring in the start of two drydockings. Lowe reported, "We operated with full utilization, taking into account the start of 2 drydockings, which amounts to 96.8% utilization overall."

  • Lowe announced a quarterly cash distribution of $0.026 per common unit, paid in August. He described progress in extending charter coverage, stating, "We made good progress in extending our charter coverage and maximizing the value of charters we already have."

  • The company completed the refinancing of Tove Knutsen via a sale and leaseback, netting $32 million in cash, and acquired the Daqing Knutsen from its sponsor for $95 million through a mix of cash and debt. The Daqing Knutsen is on time charter with PetroChina in Brazil until July 2027, with potential extension to 2032 if options are exercised.

  • Lowe detailed the initiation of a $10 million unit buyback program, under which 226,000 common units were repurchased at an average price of $7.24 per unit. He emphasized, "We deemed it prudent to increase our discretionary allocation of capital to include unit buybacks on the premise that the units trade at a significant discount to what we believe to be any reasonable valuation for the partnership and its prospects."

Outlook

  • Lowe stated that the shuttle tanker market is tightening in both Brazil and the North Sea, driven by FPSO start-ups and ramp-ups. He highlighted an extended backlog as of June 30, 2025, to $895 million of fixed contracts averaging 2.6 years, and noted that 89% of vessel time in 2026 is covered by fixed contracts. Lowe said, "We believe charter's options are likely to be taken up given the strength of the charter market."

  • The company plans to continue focusing on safe operations, high scheduled utilization, and growing earnings visibility and liquidity through medium-term chartering and opportunistic capital deployment.

Financial Results

  • Revenues for the quarter were $87.1 million, operating income was $22.2 million, net income was $6.8 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $51.6 million. Available liquidity rose to $104.8 million at quarter end, with $66.3 million in cash and $38.5 million in undrawn credit. Debt paydown continued at a rate of $95 million or more per year. Lowe stated, "Debt paydown also produces flexibility and optionality to take on leverage elsewhere to enable an accretive allocation of capital."

  • The average margin on the company’s debt was reported as 2.23% over SOFR.

Q&A

  • Liam Burke, B. Riley Securities: On the Daqing Knutsen, I know you've got customary closing events prior to taking delivery. But could you give us a sense as to when you'd expect to take delivery on that vessel? Derek Lowe: On the Daqing Knutsen, we took delivery on the day we announced it, so the 2nd of July.

  • Liam Burke, B. Riley Securities: You made the closing of the Daqing in a very shareholder-friendly manner. Do you anticipate to be able to continue to do that? Derek Lowe: We think it's unitholder-friendly whenever we do these transactions on accretive terms... But we don't have a particular timing in mind. It's obviously our financial capacity to fund any cash component that's required in the transaction. You can also see our debt schedule, what is coming up at different times and the opportunities they can present for potential releveraging or release of some sort. So the Tove sale and leaseback would be a good example of how release can happen.

  • Climent Molins, Value Investor's Edge: I wanted to ask about the older Windsor Knutsen, the Fortaleza and Recife. Could you talk a bit about how contracting discussions with potential customers compare relative to your more modern tonnage? And is there maybe any appetite to dispose of these vessels over the coming years? Derek Lowe: Our business model relates to operating vessels rather than trading them... I don't think I can expand on how those are going with -- in any individual case for commercial reasons, but we certainly are actively discussing those vessels with our clients.

  • Climent Molins, Value Investor's Edge: Could you talk a bit on how you plan to mix fleet expansion with potential distribution increases in the medium term? Derek Lowe: Fleet growth through acquisition is partly relating to growth. And the most important element of growth there is in the charter schedule. It's only through that, that we can generate income in the medium to longer term. It also helps with rejuvenating the fleet, which contributes to that as well and returns to unitholders and deployment of capital to drop-downs, we think both of those at the same time are good deployments of capital, and we don't see them as necessarily competing with each other.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a neutral to positive tone, focusing on operational execution and strategic transactions, with questions about the timing of vessel acquisitions and the approach to capital allocation.

  • Management’s tone was confident and pragmatic, emphasizing financial prudence, operational excellence, and flexibility in capital deployment. Lowe conveyed optimism about market conditions and the company’s positioning: “We're increasingly building positive momentum and taking actions on multiple fronts for the benefit of unitholders now and well into the future.”

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management maintained a similarly constructive tone, with ongoing attention to charter market dynamics and capital allocation decisions.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue increased from $84 million in Q1 to $87.1 million in Q2; net income decreased from $7.6 million to $6.8 million; adjusted EBITDA decreased slightly from $52.2 million to $51.6 million.

  • Available liquidity rose to $104 million from $101 million, and fixed contract backlog increased from $854 million to $895 million, with average contract duration extending from 2.3 years to 2.6 years.

  • The company executed a vessel acquisition (Daqing Knutsen) and initiated a unit buyback program in Q2, representing a more active approach to capital deployment than in the prior quarter.

  • Management’s tone on charter market outlook became more positive, citing tightening conditions in both Brazil and the North Sea.

  • Analysts continued to focus on fleet growth, contract renewals, and capital allocation, with a slight increase in questions regarding the balance between buybacks and acquisitions.

Risks and Concerns

  • Lowe acknowledged that timelines and contract durations are long, which can delay the financial impact of market upturns.

  • The company remains exposed to the risk of unexercised charter options and the need to secure charter renewals for vessels coming open in 2026, specifically Fortaleza and Recife.

  • Management continues to monitor debt maturities and market conditions for refinancing opportunities, noting, “any such action will be contingent on conditions at the time.”

Final Takeaway

KNOT Offshore Partners closed Q2 2025 with strong operational metrics and financial flexibility, highlighted by an increased contract backlog, a new vessel acquisition, and the launch of a buyback program. Management emphasized ongoing charter market strength in Brazil and the North Sea, prudent debt management, and a dual focus on accretive fleet growth and capital returns to unitholders as core strategic priorities for the coming quarters.
